Overall:
The following processes have been demonstrated to be effective for the removal of carbofuran from drinking water: adsorption, granular activated carbon(> 99 percent removal), membrane separation using reverse osmosis (85 to 99 percent removal), ozone (71 to 95 percent), hydrogen peroxide (45 to 90 percent), ultraviolet irradiation (50 to > 99 percent), ultraviolet irradiation with hydrogen peroxide (> 99 percent removal), and ultraviolet irradiation with ozone (up to 98 percent removal). EPA has designated granular activated carbon as the Best Available Technology for carbofuran removal. Performance of these technologies can vary based on water quality and treatment characteristics.
Conventional treatment, chlorine, and chlorine dioxide were demonstrated to provide no significant removal of carbofuran.
